article_title: Beyond AI Multiples: The Value in Boring Businesses
news_headline: Oracle Layoffs Fueled by AI, Reduces Workforce by 21,000
post_text:
Oracle just did something almost no major company has done: it told regulators, in plain language, that AI eliminated its workers. The company's annual SEC filing disclosed that 21,000 jobs — roughly 13% of its workforce — were cut because of AI adoption and deployment. Simultaneously, capex surged 162% to $55.7 billion and free cash flow cratered to negative $23.7 billion. The trade is explicit: human capital out, AI infrastructure in.

This is precisely the dynamic a recent WBR piece anticipated. While markets chase AI at stratospheric multiples, the real costs — severance bills, torched cash flow, stranded workers — are what fund those bets. Oracle is betting that future cloud revenue from OpenAI and others will vindicate the math. It might. But the gap between the AI narrative investors are pricing and the messy operational reality companies are living is widening.

Our piece 'Beyond AI Multiples: The Value in Boring Businesses' made exactly this argument. Worth revisiting.

#AIEconomy #BusinessStrategy
final_body:

Simultaneously, capex surged 162% to $55.7 billion and free cash flow cratered to negative $23.7 billion. The trade is explicit: human capital out, AI infrastructure in.

This is precisely the dynamic a recent WBR piece anticipated. While markets chase AI at stratospheric multiples, the real costs — severance bills, torched cash flow, stranded workers — are what fund those bets. Oracle is betting that future cloud revenue from OpenAI and others will vindicate the math. It might. But the gap between the AI narrative investors are pricing and the messy operational reality companies are living is widening.

Our piece 'Beyond AI Multiples: The Value in Boring Businesses' made exactly this argument. Worth revisiting.

#AIEconomy #BusinessStrategy
